165.reason: The input format specifies n (vertices) then m (edges), but the code reads m then n. This swaps the meaning of the variables.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The input format specifies n (vertices) then m (edges), but the code reads m then n. This swaps the meaning of the variables.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The input format specifies "number of vertices n and number of edges m", but the code reads m then n. This swaps the meaning of m and n throughout the program. m becomes vertex count and n becomes edge count in local scope, but global arrays and loops often rely on correct dimensions. Specifically, the loop `for(i=0;i<n;i++)` reads edges, so n is treated as edge count here. But `travelDFS(G,m)` uses m as vertex count. This inconsistency leads to incorrect traversal bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The input order is swapped: it should be n (vertices) then m (edges), but the code reads m then n. This causes all subsequent logic to use wrong values for number of vertices and edges.]
